“[Nephi] tells us he is going to give us an abbreviated account. ‘Wherefore, the things which are pleasing unto the world I do not write, but the things which are pleasing unto God and unto those who are not of the world’ (1 Ne. 6:51 Ne. 6:5). I have long noticed a widespread disease among readers of the Book of Mormon, one quite damaging to one's spiritual health. This Mormon malady is the tendency to skip, shun, or, at the very most, skim the writings of Isaiah in the Book of Mormon. This malady, or spiritual sickness, is not confined to any one particular person or group. All are potential victims: the active and less active in the…
